  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c_inst_ai_readiness", "name": "Institutional AI readiness", "aliases": ["school-level AI readiness", "school AI readiness", "organisational AI readiness"], "description": "A collective organisational capacity of a school coordinating strategy, infrastructure, governance, data and professional development for AI integration.", "grounding_span": "school-level AI readiness is associated", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_student_ai_literacy", "name": "Student AI literacy", "aliases": ["AI literacy"], "description": "A multidimensional student competency spanning understanding, applied tool use, critical evaluation and ethical reasoning about (generative) AI.", "grounding_span": "student AI literacy through aggregated teacher mechanisms", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_teacher_mechanisms", "name": "Aggregated teacher mechanisms", "aliases": ["teacher-level mechanisms", "aggregated teacher-level mechanisms"], "description": "School-shared instructional and organisational teacher perceptions aggregated to the school level as Level-2 mediators.", "grounding_span": "aggregated teacher mechanisms", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_perceived_ai_capability", "name": "Perceived AI capability", "aliases": ["teacher-perceived AI capability"], "description": "Teachers' collective perception of their instructional capacity to design and implement AI-supported pedagogy; the operative mediator.", "grounding_span": "Perceived AI Capability", "location": "Table 1"},
    {"id": "c_collective_teacher_capability", "name": "Collective teacher capability", "aliases": ["aggregated teacher capability"], "description": "The school-level collective professional capability identified as the central transmission mechanism linking readiness to literacy.", "grounding_span": "collective teacher capability as its central transmission mechanism", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_vocational_education", "name": "Vocational education", "aliases": ["vocational education and training", "VET"], "description": "The educational setting studied, at the intersection of schooling and rapidly evolving labour markets.", "grounding_span": "vocational education", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_cross_level_mediation", "name": "2-2-1 cross-level mediation", "aliases": ["cross-level mediation framework", "2-2-1 mediation model"], "description": "A mediation specification in which predictor and mediators are at Level 2 and the outcome is at Level 1.", "grounding_span": "2-2-1 cross-level", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_multilevel_ecological", "name": "Multilevel ecological process", "aliases": ["multilevel ecological framework", "nested structures"], "description": "Conception of AI integration as nested macro/meso/micro systems that interact to shape student outcomes.", "grounding_span": "multilevel ecological process", "location": "Section 2.1"},
    {"id": "c_strategic_readiness", "name": "Strategic and policy readiness", "aliases": ["strategy readiness", "strategic planning"], "description": "Readiness dimension covering strategy and policy responses to AI.", "grounding_span": "(1) strategic and policy", "location": "Section 3.2.1"},
    {"id": "c_org_environment", "name": "Organisational environment readiness", "aliases": ["structure readiness", "organisational structure"], "description": "Readiness dimension covering organisational structure and environment for AI.", "grounding_span": "(2) organisational environment", "location": "Section 3.2.1"},
    {"id": "c_process_readiness", "name": "Process readiness", "aliases": ["process guarantee"], "description": "Readiness dimension covering process guarantees such as budget, evaluation and adoption of intelligent technologies.", "grounding_span": "(3) process readiness", "location": "Section 3.2.1"},
    {"id": "c_technological_readiness", "name": "Technological readiness", "aliases": ["technical readiness", "technical environment"], "description": "Readiness dimension covering computing power, deployment and technical personnel.", "grounding_span": "(4) technological readiness", "location": "Section 3.2.1"},
    {"id": "c_data_readiness", "name": "Data readiness", "aliases": ["data preparation"], "description": "Readiness dimension covering data standardisation, integration and quality; numerically largest coefficient.", "grounding_span": "(5) data", "location": "Section 3.2.1"},
    {"id": "c_ethical_governance", "name": "Ethical governance readiness", "aliases": ["ethics readiness", "ethics and safety"], "description": "Readiness dimension covering AI risk response and safety management.", "grounding_span": "(6)ethicalgovernance", "location": "Section 3.2.1"},
    {"id": "c_perceived_positive_effects", "name": "Perceived positive instructional effects of AI", "aliases": [], "description": "Teacher perception of positive instructional impacts of generative AI.", "grounding_span": "Perceived Positive Instructional Effects of AI", "location": "Figure 3"},
    {"id": "c_perceived_negative_effects", "name": "Perceived negative instructional effects of AI", "aliases": [], "description": "Teacher perception of negative instructional impacts of generative AI.", "grounding_span": "Perceived Negative Instructional Effects of AI", "location": "Figure 3"},
    {"id": "c_perceived_difficulty", "name": "Perceived difficulty of using AI", "aliases": ["effort expectancy"], "description": "Teacher perception of effort/difficulty in using generative AI.", "grounding_span": "Perceived Difficulty of Using AI", "location": "Table 1"},
    {"id": "c_satisfaction", "name": "Satisfaction with AI use in teaching", "aliases": [], "description": "Teacher satisfaction with instructional outcomes from generative AI use.", "grounding_span": "Satisfaction with AI Use in Teaching", "location": "Table 1"},
    {"id": "c_perceived_social_support", "name": "Perceived social support for AI-enabled teaching", "aliases": ["social influence"], "description": "Teacher perception of policy/peer support for AI use in teaching.", "grounding_span": "Perceived Social Support for AI", "location": "Table 1"},
    {"id": "c_perceived_supportive_conditions", "name": "Perceived supportive conditions for AI use", "aliases": ["facilitating conditions"], "description": "Teacher perception of supportive equipment, training and technical support.", "grounding_span": "Perceived Supportive Conditions for", "location": "Table 1"},
    {"id": "c_regional_ai_development", "name": "Regional AI development", "aliases": ["regional AI industry development", "provincial AI industry competitiveness"], "description": "Contextual moderator operationalised via a provincial AI industry competitiveness index.", "grounding_span": "regional AI development", "location": "Section 3.3"},
    {"id": "c_generative_ai", "name": "Generative AI", "aliases": ["GenAI", "generative artificial intelligence"], "description": "AI that generates new content by learning from existing data; the technology whose literacy and adoption are studied.", "grounding_span": "to use generative AI in teaching", "location": "Appendix A13"},
    {"id": "c_partial_mediation", "name": "Partial mediation", "aliases": [], "description": "Mediation state in which a significant direct effect persists alongside a significant indirect effect.", "grounding_span": "indicating partial mediation", "location": "Section 4.3"},
    {"id": "c_contextual_fragility", "name": "Contextual fragility", "aliases": ["contextual robustness"], "description": "Concern that findings from advantaged environments fail to generalise; motivates robustness testing.", "grounding_span": "contextual fragility", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c_common_method_bias", "name": "Common method bias", "aliases": ["Harman's single-factor test"], "description": "Bias from single-wave self-report data, assessed via Harman's single-factor test.", "grounding_span": "common method bias is unlikely to pose a serious threat", "location": "Section 3.2.1"},

  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl_h1a", "claim_text": "School-level AI readiness is positively associated with student AI literacy after adjusting for institutional structural characteristics.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "School-level AI readiness was positively associated with student AI literacy after adjusting for", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "cl_h1b", "claim_text": "Associations between individual school-level AI readiness dimensions and students' AI literacy vary in magnitude.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "literacy vary in magnitude", "location": "Section 2.2"},
    {"id": "cl_dims_positive", "claim_text": "When examined separately, all six readiness dimensions showed positive and statistically significant associations with student AI literacy.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "all six readiness dimensions showed positive and statistically sig", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "cl_data_largest", "claim_text": "Among the readiness dimensions, data readiness showed the numerically largest standardised coefficient.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Among these, data readiness showed the", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "cl_integrated_config", "claim_text": "School AI readiness operates as an integrated configuration of mutually reinforcing components rather than independent predictors.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "school AI readiness operates as an integrated configuration of", "location": "Section 4.2"},
    {"id": "cl_six_factor", "claim_text": "The six-factor measurement model fit the data substantially better than the single-factor alternative.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "The six-factor model fit the data", "location": "Section 3.2.1"},
    {"id": "cl_icc", "claim_text": "1.18% of the total variance in student AI literacy was attributable to between-school differences.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "1.18% of the total variance in student AI literacy", "location": "Section 4.1"},
    {"id": "cl_harman", "claim_text": "The first unrotated factor explained 45.3% of the total variance, below the 50% threshold.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "the first unrotated factor explained 45.3% of the", "location": "Section 3.2.1"},
    {"id": "cl_h2", "claim_text": "Aggregated teacher-perceived AI capability partially mediates the association between institutional readiness and student AI literacy, supporting H2.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "these findings support H2 and identify", "location": "Section 4.3"},
    {"id": "cl_partial_med", "claim_text": "The direct association between school AI readiness and student AI literacy remained significant after accounting for the mediator, indicating partial mediation.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "indicating partial mediation", "location": "Section 4.3"},
    {"id": "cl_h3_no_moderation", "claim_text": "Regional AI development exerted an independent influence but did not moderate the readiness-literacy association.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "but did not moderate the readiness\u2013literacy association", "location": "Section 4.4"},
    {"id": "cl_high_region", "claim_text": "Students in high AI-development regions demonstrated higher overall AI literacy levels.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Students in high AI-development regions demonstrated higher overall AI literacy levels", "location": "Section 4.4"},
    {"id": "cl_222_replication", "claim_text": "Replication under an aggregated 2-2-2 specification provides convergent evidence that the capability-based transmission mechanism is not an artefact of cross-level modelling.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "provides convergent evidence that the capability", "location": "Section 4.4"},
    {"id": "cl_cross_sectional", "claim_text": "The design was cross-sectional, so all findings are interpreted as associations rather than causal effects.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "The design was cross-sectional", "location": "Section 3"}
  ],

  "measures": [
    {"id": "m_s1", "text": "Which description best defines generative artificial intelligence?", "response_type": "dichotomous multiple choice", "grounding_span": "Which description best defines generative", "location": "Appendix A15"},
    {"id": "m_s2", "text": "In which task can generative AI typically achieve relatively high accuracy?", "response_type": "dichotomous multiple choice", "grounding_span": "In which task can generative AI typically", "location": "Appendix A15"},
    {"id": "m_s3", "text": "What is a common challenge in prompt-based text generation?", "response_type": "dichotomous multiple choice", "grounding_span": "What is a common challenge in prompt", "location": "Appendix A15"},
    {"id": "m_s4", "text": "When using generative AI for marketing copywriting, which strategy is least likely to be effective?", "response_type": "dichotomous multiple choice", "grounding_span": "When using generative AI for marketing", "location": "Appendix A15"},
    {"id": "m_s5", "text": "A chatbot frequently provides outdated school policy information; best practice to address it.", "response_type": "dichotomous multiple choice", "grounding_span": "A chatbot frequently provides outdated", "location": "Appendix A15"},
    {"id": "m_s6", "text": "When using large language models to collect information for assignments, how should students treat the provided information?", "response_type": "dichotomous multiple choice", "grounding_span": "When using large language models to", "location": "Appendix A16"},
    {"id": "m_s7", "text": "When watching a controversial video posted by a public figure, which feature can confirm it is NOT AI-generated?", "response_type": "dichotomous multiple choice", "grounding_span": "When watching a controversial video posted", "location": "Appendix A16"},
    {"id": "m_s8", "text": "A doctor distrusts an accurate AI recommendation because the reasoning process is unclear; what core issue does this illustrate?", "response_type": "dichotomous multiple choice", "grounding_span": "A doctor distrusts an accurate AI rec", "location": "Appendix A16"},
    {"id": "m_s9", "text": "What potential copyright issue may arise when journalists use AI-generated images in commercial articles?", "response_type": "dichotomous multiple choice", "grounding_span": "What potential copyright issue may arise", "location": "Appendix A16"},
    {"id": "m_s10", "text": "Is the statement that sharing personal information with online generative AI tools poses almost no privacy risks accurate?", "response_type": "dichotomous multiple choice", "grounding_span": "almost no privacy risks", "location": "Appendix A16"},
    {"id": "m_t11", "text": "I believe that using generative AI will have positive impacts on the following aspects.", "response_type": "5-point Likert", "grounding_span": "I believe that using generative AI will have", "location": "Appendix A12"},
    {"id": "m_t13", "text": "I think using generative AI is difficult.", "response_type": "5-point Likert", "grounding_span": "I think using generative AI is difficult.", "location": "Appendix A13"},
    {"id": "m_t15", "text": "National and school-level policies encourage me to use generative AI in teaching.", "response_type": "5-point Likert", "grounding_span": "National and school-level policies encourage me", "location": "Appendix A13"},
    {"id": "m_t16", "text": "I understand that generative AI has multi-turn dialogue, contextual awareness, and self-correction capabilities.", "response_type": "5-point Likert", "grounding_span": "I understand that generative AI has multi-turn", "location": "Appendix A14"},
    {"id": "m_q11", "text": "How does your school respond to regional AI-related policies?", "response_type": "ordinal single choice", "grounding_span": "How does your school respond to regional", "location": "Appendix A2"},
    {"id": "m_q41", "text": "How is your school doing in responding to AI-related risks (technology dependence, misuse, ethical/moral risks)?", "response_type": "ordinal matrix single choice", "grounding_span": "Technology dependence risks", "location": "Appendix A7"}
  ],
